findings.title: Snapshot publication logic is duplicated across config paths
findings.impact: The proto update path manually rebuilds SampleRate objects, allocates a LogSamplerSnapshot, exchanges the atomic pointer, retires the old snapshot, and stores samplerEnabled. The flag path in log_sampler.cpp has a second hand-written version of the same publication sequence. Future changes to snapshot ownership, added sampling dimensions, or memory-ordering rules will need to be mirrored in both places, making this central concurrency-adjacent configuration code easy to drift.
findings.fix: Extract a shared helper that takes an effective LogSampleConfig or ppm triple, builds thresholds, publishes the snapshot, retires the old snapshot, and updates samplerEnabled. Have both UpdateConfigFromProto() and BuildAndPublishSnapshot() use that helper.
